requirement.text: The CGM SaMD shall alert the user when the glucose reading drops to or below 70 mg/dL within 30 seconds of the breach.

test_cases.description: Verify hypo alert fires when glucose drops to 70 mg/dL
test_cases.setup: CGM SaMD v3.2 in QA test mode; user account 'patient_01' with active subscription; approved sensor (lot SN-2026-A) on phantom arm; mobile app paired via BLE 5.0; network monitor open; glucose simulator broadcasting steady 100 mg/dL
test_cases.steps:
Step: 1. Drive simulator glucose value to 70 mg/dL.
Step: 2. Wait 30 seconds.
Step: 3. Inspect mobile-app alert UI and notification log.
test_cases.expectedResults:
ExpectedResult: 1. Glucose reading shows 70 mg/dL.
ExpectedResult: 2. Hypo alert modal appears within 30 seconds.
ExpectedResult: 3. Notification log records hypo alert with timestamp.

test_cases.description: Verify NO hypo alert at glucose just above threshold
test_cases.setup: CGM SaMD v3.2 in QA test mode; user account 'patient_01' with active subscription; approved sensor (lot SN-2026-A) on phantom arm; mobile app paired via BLE 5.0; network monitor open; simulator at 75 mg/dL
test_cases.steps:
Step: 1. Hold simulator at 75 mg/dL for 60s.
Step: 2. Inspect mobile-app alert UI.
test_cases.expectedResults:
ExpectedResult: 1. Reading shows 75 mg/dL.
ExpectedResult: 2. No hypo alert displayed.

test_cases.description: Verify alert persists until user dismisses
test_cases.setup: hypo alert active from prior test step
test_cases.steps:
Step: 1. Wait 60 seconds without input.
Step: 2. Inspect alert UI.
Step: 3. Tap 'Dismiss'.
Step: 4. Inspect alert UI again.
test_cases.expectedResults:
ExpectedResult: 1. Alert remains visible after wait.
ExpectedResult: 2. Alert closes after Dismiss tap.
ExpectedResult: 3. Notification log records acknowledgement timestamp.

decomposed_specifications.rationale: Ensures persistence of the safety signal.

test_cases.description: Verify hyper alert fires at 250 mg/dL
test_cases.setup: CGM SaMD v3.2 in QA test mode; user account 'patient_01' with active subscription; approved sensor (lot SN-2026-A) on phantom arm; mobile app paired via BLE 5.0; network monitor open; simulator broadcasting 150 mg/dL baseline
test_cases.steps:
Step: 1. Drive simulator to 250 mg/dL.
Step: 2. Wait 60 seconds.
Step: 3. Inspect alert UI and severity tag.
test_cases.expectedResults:
ExpectedResult: 1. Reading shows 250 mg/dL.
ExpectedResult: 2. Hyper alert appears within 60s.
ExpectedResult: 3. Alert severity tag reads 'Urgent'.

test_cases.description: Verify NO hyper alert at glucose just below threshold
test_cases.setup: CGM SaMD v3.2 in QA test mode; user account 'patient_01' with active subscription; approved sensor (lot SN-2026-A) on phantom arm; mobile app paired via BLE 5.0; network monitor open; simulator at 245 mg/dL
test_cases.steps:
Step: 1. Hold simulator at 245 mg/dL for 90s.
Step: 2. Inspect alert UI.
test_cases.expectedResults:
ExpectedResult: 1. Reading shows 245 mg/dL.
ExpectedResult: 2. No hyper alert displayed.

test_cases.description: Verify hyper alert is logged with severity
test_cases.setup: hyper alert active
test_cases.steps: Step: 1. Inspect notification log.
test_cases.expectedResults: ExpectedResult: 1. Log entry shows hyper alert with severity 'Urgent' and timestamp.

test_cases.setup: CGM SaMD v3.2 in QA test mode; user account 'patient_01' with active subscription; approved sensor (lot SN-2026-A) on phantom arm; mobile app paired via BLE 5.0; network monitor open; simulator broadcasting 150 mg/dL baseline
test_cases.steps:
Step: 1. Drive simulator to 240 mg/dL.
Step: 2. Wait 60 seconds.
Step: 3. Inspect alert UI and severity tag.
test_cases.expectedResults:
ExpectedResult: 1. Reading shows 240 mg/dL.
ExpectedResult: 2. Hyper alert appears within 60s.
ExpectedResult: 3. Alert severity tag reads 'Urgent'.

test_cases.description: Verify NO hyper alert at glucose just below threshold
test_cases.setup: CGM SaMD v3.2 in QA test mode; user account 'patient_01' with active subscription; approved sensor (lot SN-2026-A) on phantom arm; mobile app paired via BLE 5.0; network monitor open; simulator at 235 mg/dL
test_cases.steps:
Step: 1. Hold simulator at 235 mg/dL for 90s.
Step: 2. Inspect alert UI.
test_cases.expectedResults:
ExpectedResult: 1. Reading shows 235 mg/dL.
ExpectedResult: 2. No hyper alert displayed.

test_cases.description: Verify hyper alert is logged with severity
test_cases.setup: hyper alert active
test_cases.steps: Step: 1. Inspect notification log.
test_cases.expectedResults: ExpectedResult: 1. Log entry shows hyper alert with severity 'Urgent' and timestamp.

requirement.text: The CGM SaMD shall accept calibration entries only when the entered value is between 40 and 400 mg/dL inclusive.

test_cases.description: Verify in-range value 120 is accepted
test_cases.setup: CGM SaMD v3.2 in QA test mode; user account 'patient_01' with active subscription; approved sensor (lot SN-2026-A) on phantom arm; mobile app paired via BLE 5.0; network monitor open; calibration screen open
test_cases.steps:
Step: 1. Enter '120' into Calibration field.
Step: 2. Tap Save.
Step: 3. Inspect inline message and audit log.
test_cases.expectedResults:
ExpectedResult: 1. Value accepted with no error.
ExpectedResult: 2. Save succeeds and audit log shows new entry.

test_cases.description: Verify out-of-range value 39 is rejected
test_cases.setup: CGM SaMD v3.2 in QA test mode; user account 'patient_01' with active subscription; approved sensor (lot SN-2026-A) on phantom arm; mobile app paired via BLE 5.0; network monitor open; calibration screen open
test_cases.steps:
Step: 1. Enter '39' into Calibration field.
Step: 2. Tap Save.
Step: 3. Inspect inline error and audit log.
test_cases.expectedResults:
ExpectedResult: 1. Inline error 'Value out of range' appears.
ExpectedResult: 2. Save button disabled.
ExpectedResult: 3. Audit log unchanged.

requirement.text: The CGM SaMD shall lock all glucose readings during the first 60 minutes after sensor insertion and display 'Warming up...' to the user.

test_cases.description: Verify readings locked during warm-up
test_cases.setup: CGM SaMD v3.2 in QA test mode; user account 'patient_01' with active subscription; approved sensor (lot SN-2026-A) on phantom arm; mobile app paired via BLE 5.0; network monitor open; sensor freshly inserted at t=0
test_cases.steps:
Step: 1. Insert sensor at t=0.
Step: 2. Inspect glucose-reading area at t=1 minute.
Step: 3. Inspect status banner.
test_cases.expectedResults:
ExpectedResult: 1. Glucose reading area shows '--' (locked).
ExpectedResult: 2. Status banner reads 'Warming up...'.

test_cases.description: Verify reading resumes immediately after warm-up at t=60m+1s
test_cases.setup: CGM SaMD v3.2 in QA test mode; user account 'patient_01' with active subscription; approved sensor (lot SN-2026-A) on phantom arm; mobile app paired via BLE 5.0; network monitor open; sensor just past warm-up
test_cases.steps:
Step: 1. Wait until t=60 minutes 1 second.
Step: 2. Inspect glucose-reading area.
test_cases.expectedResults:
ExpectedResult: 1. Glucose value displayed (numeric, mg/dL).
ExpectedResult: 2. Status banner cleared.

test_cases.description: Verify reading is NOT displayed at t=60m-1s (boundary)
test_cases.setup: CGM SaMD v3.2 in QA test mode; user account 'patient_01' with active subscription; approved sensor (lot SN-2026-A) on phantom arm; mobile app paired via BLE 5.0; network monitor open; sensor near end of warm-up
test_cases.steps:
Step: 1. Wait until t=60 minutes minus 1 second.
Step: 2. Inspect glucose-reading area.
test_cases.expectedResults:
ExpectedResult: 1. Glucose reading area still shows '--'.
ExpectedResult: 2. Status banner still reads 'Warming up...'.

requirement.text: The CGM SaMD shall lock all glucose readings during the first 120 minutes after sensor insertion and display 'Warming up...' to the user.

test_cases.description: Verify readings locked during warm-up
test_cases.setup: CGM SaMD v3.2 in QA test mode; user account 'patient_01' with active subscription; approved sensor (lot SN-2026-A) on phantom arm; mobile app paired via BLE 5.0; network monitor open; sensor freshly inserted at t=0
test_cases.steps:
Step: 1. Insert sensor at t=0.
Step: 2. Inspect glucose-reading area at t=1 minute.
Step: 3. Inspect status banner.
test_cases.expectedResults:
ExpectedResult: 1. Glucose reading area shows '--' (locked).
ExpectedResult: 2. Status banner reads 'Warming up...'.

test_cases.description: Verify reading resumes immediately after warm-up at t=120m+1s
test_cases.setup: CGM SaMD v3.2 in QA test mode; user account 'patient_01' with active subscription; approved sensor (lot SN-2026-A) on phantom arm; mobile app paired via BLE 5.0; network monitor open; sensor just past warm-up
test_cases.steps:
Step: 1. Wait until t=120 minutes 1 second.
Step: 2. Inspect glucose-reading area.
test_cases.expectedResults:
ExpectedResult: 1. Glucose value displayed (numeric, mg/dL).
ExpectedResult: 2. Status banner cleared.

test_cases.description: Verify reading is NOT displayed at t=120m-1s (boundary)
test_cases.setup: CGM SaMD v3.2 in QA test mode; user account 'patient_01' with active subscription; approved sensor (lot SN-2026-A) on phantom arm; mobile app paired via BLE 5.0; network monitor open; sensor near end of warm-up
test_cases.steps:
Step: 1. Wait until t=120 minutes minus 1 second.
Step: 2. Inspect glucose-reading area.
test_cases.expectedResults:
ExpectedResult: 1. Glucose reading area still shows '--'.
ExpectedResult: 2. Status banner still reads 'Warming up...'.

requirement.text: The CGM transmitter shall display a low-battery warning to the user when battery level drops to or below 20 percent.

test_cases.description: Verify warning fires at 20% battery
test_cases.setup: CGM SaMD v3.2 in QA test mode; user account 'patient_01' with active subscription; approved sensor (lot SN-2026-A) on phantom arm; mobile app paired via BLE 5.0; network monitor open; battery simulator at 25%
test_cases.steps:
Step: 1. Drive battery simulator to 20%.
Step: 2. Wait 10 seconds.
Step: 3. Inspect warning UI and glucose-reading area.
test_cases.expectedResults:
ExpectedResult: 1. Battery indicator shows 20%.
ExpectedResult: 2. Low-battery warning visible.
ExpectedResult: 3. Glucose reading still updating.

test_cases.description: Verify NO warning at 25% (just above threshold)
test_cases.setup: CGM SaMD v3.2 in QA test mode; user account 'patient_01' with active subscription; approved sensor (lot SN-2026-A) on phantom arm; mobile app paired via BLE 5.0; network monitor open; battery at 25%
test_cases.steps:
Step: 1. Hold battery at 25%.
Step: 2. Inspect UI.
test_cases.expectedResults:
ExpectedResult: 1. No low-battery warning visible.
ExpectedResult: 2. Glucose reading area normal.

test_cases.description: Verify glucose readings continue while warning displayed
test_cases.setup: low-battery warning active
test_cases.steps: Step: 1. Inspect glucose-reading area for 60 seconds.
test_cases.expectedResults: ExpectedResult: 1. Glucose reading updates continuously (no '--' lockout).

test_cases.setup: CGM SaMD v3.2 in QA test mode; user account 'patient_01' with active subscription; approved sensor (lot SN-2026-A) on phantom arm; mobile app paired via BLE 5.0; network monitor open; battery simulator at 10%
test_cases.steps:
Step: 1. Drive battery simulator to 5%.
Step: 2. Wait 10 seconds.
Step: 3. Inspect warning UI and glucose-reading area.
test_cases.expectedResults:
ExpectedResult: 1. Battery indicator shows 5%.
ExpectedResult: 2. Low-battery warning visible.
ExpectedResult: 3. Glucose reading still updating.

test_cases.description: Verify NO warning at 10% (just above threshold)
test_cases.setup: CGM SaMD v3.2 in QA test mode; user account 'patient_01' with active subscription; approved sensor (lot SN-2026-A) on phantom arm; mobile app paired via BLE 5.0; network monitor open; battery at 10%
test_cases.steps:
Step: 1. Hold battery at 10%.
Step: 2. Inspect UI.
test_cases.expectedResults:
ExpectedResult: 1. No low-battery warning visible.
ExpectedResult: 2. Glucose reading area normal.

test_cases.description: Verify glucose readings continue while warning displayed
test_cases.setup: low-battery warning active
test_cases.steps: Step: 1. Inspect glucose-reading area for 60 seconds.
test_cases.expectedResults: ExpectedResult: 1. Glucose reading updates continuously (no '--' lockout).

summary.objective: Verify warning fires at 5% battery
protocol: Step: 3. Inspect warning UI and glucose-reading area.

summary.objective: Verify NO warning at 10% (just above threshold)
Verify glucose readings continue while warning displayed
protocol: Step: 1. Inspect glucose-reading area for 60 seconds.
acceptance_criteria: ExpectedResult: 1. Glucose reading updates continuously (no '--' lockout).

requirement.text: The CGM mobile app shall complete BLE pairing with the transmitter within 30 seconds or fail with a timeout error.

test_cases.description: Verify pairing succeeds within 30 seconds
test_cases.setup: CGM SaMD v3.2 in QA test mode; user account 'patient_01' with active subscription; approved sensor (lot SN-2026-A) on phantom arm; mobile app paired via BLE 5.0; network monitor open; transmitter in pairing mode, RSSI -60 dBm
test_cases.steps:
Step: 1. Tap 'Pair Transmitter' in app.
Step: 2. Wait up to 30 seconds.
Step: 3. Inspect connection status.
test_cases.expectedResults:
ExpectedResult: 1. Status updates to 'Connected' before 30s.
ExpectedResult: 2. Transmitter MAC shown in connection panel.

test_cases.description: Verify timeout error when transmitter unreachable
test_cases.setup: CGM SaMD v3.2 in QA test mode; user account 'patient_01' with active subscription; approved sensor (lot SN-2026-A) on phantom arm; mobile app paired via BLE 5.0; network monitor open; transmitter powered off
test_cases.steps:
Step: 1. Tap 'Pair Transmitter' in app.
Step: 2. Wait 35 seconds.
Step: 3. Inspect error UI and BLE scan handle.
test_cases.expectedResults:
ExpectedResult: 1. After 30s, error 'Pairing timeout' displayed.
ExpectedResult: 2. BLE scan handle released (no active callback in debug log).

test_cases.description: Verify retry available after timeout
test_cases.setup: timeout error shown
test_cases.steps:
Step: 1. Tap 'Retry'.
Step: 2. Inspect status.
test_cases.expectedResults:
ExpectedResult: 1. New pairing attempt initiated.
ExpectedResult: 2. Status returns to 'Searching...'.

requirement.text: The CGM mobile app shall sync glucose readings to the cloud every 5 minutes when the device is online, and retry once on transient failure.

test_cases.description: Verify sync request fires at exactly 5-minute interval
test_cases.setup: CGM SaMD v3.2 in QA test mode; user account 'patient_01' with active subscription; approved sensor (lot SN-2026-A) on phantom arm; mobile app paired via BLE 5.0; network monitor open; cloud endpoint reachable, network monitor recording
test_cases.steps:
Step: 1. Reset session at t=0.
Step: 2. Wait 5 minutes.
Step: 3. Inspect network monitor for outbound POST to /sync.
test_cases.expectedResults:
ExpectedResult: 1. POST /sync request observed at t=5m +/- 5s.
ExpectedResult: 2. Response 200 OK.

test_cases.description: Verify retry on transient 503 failure
test_cases.setup: CGM SaMD v3.2 in QA test mode; user account 'patient_01' with active subscription; approved sensor (lot SN-2026-A) on phantom arm; mobile app paired via BLE 5.0; network monitor open; cloud endpoint stub returns 503 once then 200
test_cases.steps:
Step: 1. Trigger sync via test hook.
Step: 2. Inspect network monitor for retry.
test_cases.expectedResults:
ExpectedResult: 1. First POST returns 503.
ExpectedResult: 2. Retry POST issued within 60s.
ExpectedResult: 3. Second response 200 OK.

test_cases.description: Verify no sync attempted when offline
test_cases.setup: CGM SaMD v3.2 in QA test mode; user account 'patient_01' with active subscription; approved sensor (lot SN-2026-A) on phantom arm; mobile app paired via BLE 5.0; network monitor open; network disabled (airplane mode)
test_cases.steps:
Step: 1. Wait 6 minutes in airplane mode.
Step: 2. Inspect network monitor.
test_cases.expectedResults:
ExpectedResult: 1. Zero POST /sync attempts during window.
ExpectedResult: 2. Local sync queue length increases.

requirement.text: The CGM SaMD shall retain audit-log entries (calibration, alert acknowledgement, user login) for at least 7 years before any purge is permitted.

test_cases.description: Verify 7-year-old entry still queryable
test_cases.setup: CGM SaMD v3.2 in QA test mode; user account 'patient_01' with active subscription; approved sensor (lot SN-2026-A) on phantom arm; mobile app paired via BLE 5.0; network monitor open; audit DB seeded with entry dated 6 years ago
test_cases.steps:
Step: 1. Issue audit-query API call for entry.
Step: 2. Inspect response payload.
test_cases.expectedResults:
ExpectedResult: 1. Response 200 with entry payload.
ExpectedResult: 2. Timestamp matches seeded value.

test_cases.description: Verify purge rejected for under-retention entry
test_cases.setup: CGM SaMD v3.2 in QA test mode; user account 'patient_01' with active subscription; approved sensor (lot SN-2026-A) on phantom arm; mobile app paired via BLE 5.0; network monitor open; audit DB has 1-year-old entry
test_cases.steps:
Step: 1. Issue admin purge call for entry.
Step: 2. Inspect response and DB state.
test_cases.expectedResults:
ExpectedResult: 1. Response 403 'Retention policy violation'.
ExpectedResult: 2. Entry still present in DB.

test_cases.description: Verify purge succeeds for over-retention entry
test_cases.setup: CGM SaMD v3.2 in QA test mode; user account 'patient_01' with active subscription; approved sensor (lot SN-2026-A) on phantom arm; mobile app paired via BLE 5.0; network monitor open; audit DB has entry dated 8 years ago
test_cases.steps:
Step: 1. Issue admin purge call for entry.
Step: 2. Inspect response and DB state.
test_cases.expectedResults:
ExpectedResult: 1. Response 200 'Purged'.
ExpectedResult: 2. Entry removed from DB.

decomposed_specifications.acceptance_criteria: Purge succeeds for entries timestamped >7 years ago.

requirement.text: The CGM SaMD shall retain audit-log entries (calibration, alert acknowledgement, user login) for at least 10 years before any purge is permitted.

test_cases.description: Verify 10-year-old entry still queryable
test_cases.setup: CGM SaMD v3.2 in QA test mode; user account 'patient_01' with active subscription; approved sensor (lot SN-2026-A) on phantom arm; mobile app paired via BLE 5.0; network monitor open; audit DB seeded with entry dated 9 years ago
test_cases.steps:
Step: 1. Issue audit-query API call for entry.
Step: 2. Inspect response payload.
test_cases.expectedResults:
ExpectedResult: 1. Response 200 with entry payload.
ExpectedResult: 2. Timestamp matches seeded value.

test_cases.description: Verify purge rejected for under-retention entry
test_cases.setup: CGM SaMD v3.2 in QA test mode; user account 'patient_01' with active subscription; approved sensor (lot SN-2026-A) on phantom arm; mobile app paired via BLE 5.0; network monitor open; audit DB has 1-year-old entry
test_cases.steps:
Step: 1. Issue admin purge call for entry.
Step: 2. Inspect response and DB state.
test_cases.expectedResults:
ExpectedResult: 1. Response 403 'Retention policy violation'.
ExpectedResult: 2. Entry still present in DB.

test_cases.description: Verify purge succeeds for over-retention entry
test_cases.setup: CGM SaMD v3.2 in QA test mode; user account 'patient_01' with active subscription; approved sensor (lot SN-2026-A) on phantom arm; mobile app paired via BLE 5.0; network monitor open; audit DB has entry dated 11 years ago
test_cases.steps:
Step: 1. Issue admin purge call for entry.
Step: 2. Inspect response and DB state.
test_cases.expectedResults:
ExpectedResult: 1. Response 200 'Purged'.
ExpectedResult: 2. Entry removed from DB.

requirement.text: The CGM SaMD shall transmit glucose readings to the paired insulin pump every 60 seconds when both devices are linked.

test_cases.description: Verify reading transmitted every 60 seconds
test_cases.setup: CGM SaMD v3.2 in QA test mode; user account 'patient_01' with active subscription; approved sensor (lot SN-2026-A) on phantom arm; mobile app paired via BLE 5.0; network monitor open; paired pump in test bench, BLE link active
test_cases.steps:
Step: 1. Reset pump receive log.
Step: 2. Wait 300 seconds.
Step: 3. Inspect pump receive log.
test_cases.expectedResults:
ExpectedResult: 1. Pump receives readings at intervals of 60s +/- 2s.
ExpectedResult: 2. Reading values match CGM-app values.

test_cases.description: Verify transmission pauses when pump unlinks
test_cases.setup: CGM SaMD v3.2 in QA test mode; user account 'patient_01' with active subscription; approved sensor (lot SN-2026-A) on phantom arm; mobile app paired via BLE 5.0; network monitor open; paired pump powered off
test_cases.steps:
Step: 1. Power off pump.
Step: 2. Wait 60 seconds.
Step: 3. Inspect outbound BLE log on phone.
test_cases.expectedResults:
ExpectedResult: 1. After link drops, zero outbound transmissions to pump address.
ExpectedResult: 2. Link state reported as 'disconnected'.

test_cases.description: Verify transmission resumes after pump reconnect
test_cases.setup: pump powered back on after step above
test_cases.steps:
Step: 1. Power pump on.
Step: 2. Wait 90 seconds.
Step: 3. Inspect pump receive log.
test_cases.expectedResults:
ExpectedResult: 1. Pump receives at least one reading within 90s.
ExpectedResult: 2. Cadence resumes at 60s.

requirement.text: The CGM SaMD shall transmit glucose readings to the paired insulin pump every 60 seconds when both devices are linked.

decomposed_specifications.description: Transmit reading to pump every 60 seconds
decomposed_specifications.acceptance_criteria: Pump receives reading at intervals of 60s +/- 2s.

decomposed_specifications.description: Pause transmission when pump link is broken
decomposed_specifications.acceptance_criteria: No reading transmitted while link state = 'disconnected'.

decomposed_specifications.description: Resume transmission when pump reconnects
decomposed_specifications.acceptance_criteria: Transmission cadence restarts within 1 interval after reconnect.

requirement.text: The CGM SaMD shall predict hypoglycemic events 20 minutes in advance with a sensitivity of at least 80 percent across all monitored sessions.

test_cases.description: Verify predictive alert fires 20 minutes before hypo breach
test_cases.setup: CGM SaMD v3.2 in QA test mode; user account 'patient_01' with active subscription; approved sensor (lot SN-2026-A) on phantom arm; mobile app paired via BLE 5.0; network monitor open; simulator running pre-recorded hypo event trace
test_cases.steps:
Step: 1. Start trace at t=0.
Step: 2. Note alert timestamps.
Step: 3. Note actual breach timestamp.
test_cases.expectedResults:
ExpectedResult: 1. Predictive alert fires 20m +/- 1m before actual breach.
ExpectedResult: 2. Alert tagged 'Predictive'.

test_cases.description: Verify session sensitivity meets 80% threshold
test_cases.setup: CGM SaMD v3.2 in QA test mode; user account 'patient_01' with active subscription; approved sensor (lot SN-2026-A) on phantom arm; mobile app paired via BLE 5.0; network monitor open; trace with 10 hypo events over 1 hour
test_cases.steps:
Step: 1. Run trace to completion.
Step: 2. Compare predicted vs actual events.
Step: 3. Compute sensitivity.
test_cases.expectedResults:
ExpectedResult: 1. Predicted at least 8 of 10 events.
ExpectedResult: 2. Computed sensitivity >= 80%.

test_cases.description: Verify suppression when sensor noise high
test_cases.setup: CGM SaMD v3.2 in QA test mode; user account 'patient_01' with active subscription; approved sensor (lot SN-2026-A) on phantom arm; mobile app paired via BLE 5.0; network monitor open; simulator injecting high-noise signal (15 mg/dL std-dev)
test_cases.steps:
Step: 1. Start noisy trace.
Step: 2. Note any predictive alerts.
test_cases.expectedResults:
ExpectedResult: 1. Zero predictive alerts during noisy window.
ExpectedResult: 2. Status banner reads 'Sensor signal noisy'.

test_cases.setup: CGM SaMD v3.2 in QA test mode; user account 'patient_01' with active subscription; approved sensor (lot SN-2026-A) on phantom arm; mobile app paired via BLE 5.0; network monitor open; simulator running pre-recorded hypo event trace
test_cases.steps:
Step: 1. Start trace at t=0.
Step: 2. Note alert timestamps.
Step: 3. Note actual breach timestamp.
test_cases.expectedResults:
ExpectedResult: 1. Predictive alert fires 10m +/- 1m before actual breach.
ExpectedResult: 2. Alert tagged 'Predictive'.

test_cases.description: Verify session sensitivity meets 90% threshold
test_cases.setup: CGM SaMD v3.2 in QA test mode; user account 'patient_01' with active subscription; approved sensor (lot SN-2026-A) on phantom arm; mobile app paired via BLE 5.0; network monitor open; trace with 10 hypo events over 1 hour
test_cases.steps:
Step: 1. Run trace to completion.
Step: 2. Compare predicted vs actual events.
Step: 3. Compute sensitivity.
test_cases.expectedResults:
ExpectedResult: 1. Predicted at least 9 of 10 events.
ExpectedResult: 2. Computed sensitivity >= 90%.

test_cases.description: Verify suppression when sensor noise high
test_cases.setup: CGM SaMD v3.2 in QA test mode; user account 'patient_01' with active subscription; approved sensor (lot SN-2026-A) on phantom arm; mobile app paired via BLE 5.0; network monitor open; simulator injecting high-noise signal (15 mg/dL std-dev)
test_cases.steps:
Step: 1. Start noisy trace.
Step: 2. Note any predictive alerts.
test_cases.expectedResults:
ExpectedResult: 1. Zero predictive alerts during noisy window.
ExpectedResult: 2. Status banner reads 'Sensor signal noisy'.

requirement.text: The CGM SaMD shall predict hypoglycemic events 10 minutes in advance with a sensitivity of at least 90 percent across all monitored sessions.

decomposed_specifications.description: Predict hypo 10 minutes ahead of breach
decomposed_specifications.acceptance_criteria: Predictive alert fires 10m before glucose <= 70 mg/dL.

decomposed_specifications.description: Maintain sensitivity >= 90% across the test session
decomposed_specifications.acceptance_criteria: True-positive rate over 1-hour session >= 90%.

decomposed_specifications.description: Suppress prediction when sensor data quality is poor
decomposed_specifications.acceptance_criteria: No prediction issued when sensor noise > 10 mg/dL std-dev.

test-case vocabulary diverges from requirement vocabulary in one or more steps

requirement.text: The CGM SaMD shall send an SMS notification to all enrolled caregivers when the user's glucose drops to or below 55 mg/dL for more than 5 consecutive minutes.

test_cases.description: Verify SMS sent after 5+ minutes at 55 mg/dL
test_cases.setup: CGM SaMD v3.2 in QA test mode; user account 'patient_01' with active subscription; approved sensor (lot SN-2026-A) on phantom arm; mobile app paired via BLE 5.0; network monitor open; 3 enrolled caregivers, SMS gateway in test mode, simulator at 95 mg/dL
test_cases.steps:
Step: 1. Drive simulator to 55 mg/dL.
Step: 2. Hold for 6 minutes.
Step: 3. Inspect SMS gateway log.
test_cases.expectedResults:
ExpectedResult: 1. After 5m at 55 mg/dL, SMS issued.
ExpectedResult: 2. All 3 enrolled phones receive SMS within 60s.
ExpectedResult: 3. Gateway log shows 3 delivered entries.

test_cases.description: Verify NO SMS when breach lasts under 5 minutes
test_cases.setup: CGM SaMD v3.2 in QA test mode; user account 'patient_01' with active subscription; approved sensor (lot SN-2026-A) on phantom arm; mobile app paired via BLE 5.0; network monitor open; simulator at 55 mg/dL
test_cases.steps:
Step: 1. Drive simulator to 55 mg/dL.
Step: 2. After 3 minutes, drive simulator to 100 mg/dL.
Step: 3. Inspect SMS gateway log.
test_cases.expectedResults:
ExpectedResult: 1. Zero SMS issued during the brief breach.
ExpectedResult: 2. Gateway log unchanged.

test_cases.description: Verify delivery-failure log entry recorded
test_cases.setup: CGM SaMD v3.2 in QA test mode; user account 'patient_01' with active subscription; approved sensor (lot SN-2026-A) on phantom arm; mobile app paired via BLE 5.0; network monitor open; one caregiver phone unreachable (carrier 503)
test_cases.steps:
Step: 1. Trigger urgent SMS path.
Step: 2. Inspect delivery log.
test_cases.expectedResults:
ExpectedResult: 1. Two entries 'delivered' and one 'failed' in log.
ExpectedResult: 2. Failed entry includes carrier error code.

requirement.text: The CGM SaMD shall send an SMS notification to all enrolled caregivers when the user's glucose drops to or below 70 mg/dL for more than 15 consecutive minutes.

test_cases.description: Verify SMS sent after 15+ minutes at 70 mg/dL
test_cases.setup: CGM SaMD v3.2 in QA test mode; user account 'patient_01' with active subscription; approved sensor (lot SN-2026-A) on phantom arm; mobile app paired via BLE 5.0; network monitor open; 3 enrolled caregivers, SMS gateway in test mode, simulator at 95 mg/dL
test_cases.steps:
Step: 1. Drive simulator to 70 mg/dL.
Step: 2. Hold for 16 minutes.
Step: 3. Inspect SMS gateway log.
test_cases.expectedResults:
ExpectedResult: 1. After 15m at 70 mg/dL, SMS issued.
ExpectedResult: 2. All 3 enrolled phones receive SMS within 60s.
ExpectedResult: 3. Gateway log shows 3 delivered entries.

test_cases.description: Verify NO SMS when breach lasts under 15 minutes
test_cases.setup: CGM SaMD v3.2 in QA test mode; user account 'patient_01' with active subscription; approved sensor (lot SN-2026-A) on phantom arm; mobile app paired via BLE 5.0; network monitor open; simulator at 70 mg/dL
test_cases.steps:
Step: 1. Drive simulator to 70 mg/dL.
Step: 2. After 10 minutes, drive simulator to 100 mg/dL.
Step: 3. Inspect SMS gateway log.
test_cases.expectedResults:
ExpectedResult: 1. Zero SMS issued during the brief breach.
ExpectedResult: 2. Gateway log unchanged.

requirement.text: The CGM SaMD shall send an SMS notification to all enrolled caregivers when the user's glucose drops to or below 65 mg/dL for more than 5 consecutive minutes.

test_cases.description: Verify SMS sent after 5+ minutes at 65 mg/dL
test_cases.setup: CGM SaMD v3.2 in QA test mode; user account 'patient_01' with active subscription; approved sensor (lot SN-2026-A) on phantom arm; mobile app paired via BLE 5.0; network monitor open; 3 enrolled caregivers, SMS gateway in test mode, simulator at 95 mg/dL
test_cases.steps:
Step: 1. Drive simulator to 65 mg/dL.
Step: 2. Hold for 6 minutes.
Step: 3. Inspect SMS gateway log.
test_cases.expectedResults:
ExpectedResult: 1. After 5m at 65 mg/dL, SMS issued.
ExpectedResult: 2. All 3 enrolled phones receive SMS within 60s.
ExpectedResult: 3. Gateway log shows 3 delivered entries.

test_cases.description: Verify NO SMS when breach lasts under 5 minutes
test_cases.setup: CGM SaMD v3.2 in QA test mode; user account 'patient_01' with active subscription; approved sensor (lot SN-2026-A) on phantom arm; mobile app paired via BLE 5.0; network monitor open; simulator at 65 mg/dL
test_cases.steps:
Step: 1. Drive simulator to 65 mg/dL.
Step: 2. After 4 minutes, drive simulator to 100 mg/dL.
Step: 3. Inspect SMS gateway log.
test_cases.expectedResults:
ExpectedResult: 1. Zero SMS issued during the brief breach.
ExpectedResult: 2. Gateway log unchanged.

summary.objective: Verify SMS sent after 5+ minutes at 65 mg/dL
acceptance_criteria: ExpectedResult: 1. After 5m at 65 mg/dL, SMS issued.
ExpectedResult: 2. All 3 enrolled phones receive SMS within 60s.
ExpectedResult: 3. Gateway log shows 3 delivered entries.

summary.objective: Verify NO SMS when breach lasts under 5 minutes
protocol: Step: 2. After 4 minutes, drive simulator to 100 mg/dL.
acceptance_criteria: ExpectedResult: 1. Zero SMS issued during the brief breach.

test_cases.description: Verify NO SMS when breach lasts under 15 minutes
test_cases.setup: CGM SaMD v3.2 in QA test mode; user account 'patient_01' with active subscription; approved sensor (lot SN-2026-A) on phantom arm; mobile app paired via BLE 5.0; network monitor open; simulator at 70 mg/dL

test_cases.description: Verify delivery-failure log entry recorded
test_cases.setup: CGM SaMD v3.2 in QA test mode; user account 'patient_01' with active subscription; approved sensor (lot SN-2026-A) on phantom arm; mobile app paired via BLE 5.0; network monitor open; one caregiver phone unreachable (carrier 503)
test_cases.steps:
Step: 1. Trigger urgent SMS path.
Step: 2. Inspect delivery log.
test_cases.expectedResults:
ExpectedResult: 1. Two entries 'delivered' and one 'failed' in log.
ExpectedResult: 2. Failed entry includes carrier error code.

requirement.text: The CGM SaMD shall compute and display the user's time-in-range (70 to 180 mg/dL inclusive) percentage over the most recent 24-hour window.

test_cases.description: Verify TIR computed correctly for 24h with mix of in/out range
test_cases.setup: CGM SaMD v3.2 in QA test mode; user account 'patient_01' with active subscription; approved sensor (lot SN-2026-A) on phantom arm; mobile app paired via BLE 5.0; network monitor open; synthetic trace: 70% samples in [70, 180]
test_cases.steps:
Step: 1. Run trace through full 24h window.
Step: 2. Inspect TIR display and computed value.
test_cases.expectedResults:
ExpectedResult: 1. Display shows '70.0%'.
ExpectedResult: 2. Computed value matches input ratio.

test_cases.description: Verify boundary samples at 70 and 180 mg/dL counted as in-range
test_cases.setup: CGM SaMD v3.2 in QA test mode; user account 'patient_01' with active subscription; approved sensor (lot SN-2026-A) on phantom arm; mobile app paired via BLE 5.0; network monitor open; synthetic trace: all samples at exactly 70 or 180 mg/dL
test_cases.steps:
Step: 1. Run trace.
Step: 2. Inspect TIR display.
test_cases.expectedResults:
ExpectedResult: 1. Display shows '100.0%'.
ExpectedResult: 2. Boundary samples counted as in-range.

requirement.text: The CGM SaMD shall compute and display the user's time-in-range (70 to 180 mg/dL inclusive) percentage over the most recent 24-hour window.

decomposed_specifications.description: Maintain a rolling 24-hour window of glucose readings
decomposed_specifications.acceptance_criteria: Window contains last 24h of readings, FIFO.

decomposed_specifications.description: Compute time-in-range percentage from window samples
decomposed_specifications.acceptance_criteria: TIR% = (count of samples in [70, 180]) / total * 100.

decomposed_specifications.acceptance_criteria: UI value matches computed value to within 0.1%.

test_cases.setup: CGM SaMD v3.2 in QA test mode; user account 'patient_01' with active subscription; approved sensor (lot SN-2026-A) on phantom arm; mobile app paired via BLE 5.0; network monitor open; synthetic trace: 70% samples in [70, 180]
test_cases.steps:
Step: 1. Run trace through full 12h window.
Step: 2. Inspect TIR display and computed value.
test_cases.expectedResults:
ExpectedResult: 1. Display shows '70.0%'.
ExpectedResult: 2. Computed value matches input ratio.

Step: 2. Inspect TIR display.
test_cases.expectedResults:

test_cases.steps:

Step: 2. Inspect readings UI.
test_cases.expectedResults:

ExpectedResult: 2. No replacement banner shown.

requirement.text: The CGM mobile app shall support up to 5 distinct user accounts on a single device installation and reject creation of an additional account beyond the cap.

test_cases.description: Verify 5th account is allowed
test_cases.setup: CGM SaMD v3.2 in QA test mode; user account 'patient_01' with active subscription; approved sensor (lot SN-2026-A) on phantom arm; mobile app paired via BLE 5.0; network monitor open; device with 4 existing accounts
test_cases.steps:
Step: 1. Create new account.
Step: 2. Inspect account list.
test_cases.expectedResults:
ExpectedResult: 1. Account creation succeeds.
ExpectedResult: 2. Account list shows 5 entries.

test_cases.description: Verify 6th account is rejected
test_cases.setup: CGM SaMD v3.2 in QA test mode; user account 'patient_01' with active subscription; approved sensor (lot SN-2026-A) on phantom arm; mobile app paired via BLE 5.0; network monitor open; device with 5 existing accounts
test_cases.steps:
Step: 1. Attempt to create new account.
Step: 2. Inspect error UI.
test_cases.expectedResults:
ExpectedResult: 1. Error 'Account limit reached' shown.
ExpectedResult: 2. Account list still shows 5 entries.

test_cases.description: Verify deletion permits re-creation
test_cases.setup: CGM SaMD v3.2 in QA test mode; user account 'patient_01' with active subscription; approved sensor (lot SN-2026-A) on phantom arm; mobile app paired via BLE 5.0; network monitor open; device at cap with 5 accounts
test_cases.steps:
Step: 1. Delete one account.
Step: 2. Create new account.
Step: 3. Inspect account list.
test_cases.expectedResults:
ExpectedResult: 1. Deletion succeeds.
ExpectedResult: 2. New creation succeeds.
ExpectedResult: 3. List again shows 5 entries.

requirement.text: The CGM mobile app shall support up to 5 distinct user accounts on a single device installation and reject creation of an additional account beyond the cap.

decomposed_specifications.acceptance_criteria: Creation API returns error when current count = 5.

decomposed_specifications.acceptance_criteria: After deleting any account, creation succeeds.

requirement.text: The CGM SaMD shall send glucose data and user location to the user's pre-registered emergency contact when severe hypoglycemia (glucose <= 50 mg/dL) is sustained for more than 5 consecutive minutes without user acknowledgement.

test_cases.description: Verify dispatch after 5+ min at 50 mg/dL
test_cases.setup: CGM SaMD v3.2 in QA test mode; user account 'patient_01' with active subscription; approved sensor (lot SN-2026-A) on phantom arm; mobile app paired via BLE 5.0; network monitor open; registered emergency contact, GPS unlocked, simulator at 80 mg/dL
test_cases.steps:
Step: 1. Drive simulator to 50 mg/dL.
Step: 2. Hold for 6 minutes without user input.
Step: 3. Inspect emergency endpoint log.
test_cases.expectedResults:
ExpectedResult: 1. POST observed at 5m+1m mark.
ExpectedResult: 2. Payload contains glucose history, GPS coords, contact name.

test_cases.description: Verify NO dispatch when user taps 'I'm safe' within window
test_cases.setup: CGM SaMD v3.2 in QA test mode; user account 'patient_01' with active subscription; approved sensor (lot SN-2026-A) on phantom arm; mobile app paired via BLE 5.0; network monitor open; simulator at 50 mg/dL
test_cases.steps:
Step: 1. Drive simulator to 50 mg/dL.
Step: 2. After 3 minutes, tap 'I'm safe'.
Step: 3. Inspect emergency endpoint log.
test_cases.expectedResults:
ExpectedResult: 1. Zero POST observed during window.
ExpectedResult: 2. UI banner clears.

test_cases.description: Verify dispatch payload schema
test_cases.setup: dispatch fired in prior step
test_cases.steps: Step: 1. Inspect emergency endpoint log payload.
test_cases.expectedResults: ExpectedResult: 1. Payload includes glucose_history (>=20 samples), gps_lat, gps_lon, contact_name fields.

test_cases.setup: CGM SaMD v3.2 in QA test mode; user account 'patient_01' with active subscription; approved sensor (lot SN-2026-A) on phantom arm; mobile app paired via BLE 5.0; network monitor open; registered emergency contact, GPS unlocked, simulator at 80 mg/dL
test_cases.steps:
Step: 1. Drive simulator to 45 mg/dL.
Step: 2. Hold for 6 minutes without user input.
Step: 3. Inspect emergency endpoint log.
test_cases.expectedResults:
ExpectedResult: 1. POST observed at 5m+1m mark.
ExpectedResult: 2. Payload contains glucose history, GPS coords, contact name.

Step: 2. After 4 minutes, tap 'I'm safe'.
Step: 3. Inspect emergency endpoint log.
test_cases.expectedResults:

ExpectedResult: 2. UI banner clears.

requirement.text: The CGM SaMD shall display a downward trend arrow when glucose decreases at a rate of 2 mg/dL per minute or faster, and clear the arrow when the rate falls below the threshold.

test_cases.description: Verify arrow appears at rate of -2 mg/dL/min
test_cases.setup: CGM SaMD v3.2 in QA test mode; user account 'patient_01' with active subscription; approved sensor (lot SN-2026-A) on phantom arm; mobile app paired via BLE 5.0; network monitor open; simulator descending at -2 mg/dL/min from 150
test_cases.steps:
Step: 1. Run trace for 5 minutes.
Step: 2. Inspect rate field and arrow display.
test_cases.expectedResults:
ExpectedResult: 1. Computed rate = -2 mg/dL/min.
ExpectedResult: 2. Downward arrow visible.

test_cases.description: Verify NO arrow at rate of -1 mg/dL/min
test_cases.setup: CGM SaMD v3.2 in QA test mode; user account 'patient_01' with active subscription; approved sensor (lot SN-2026-A) on phantom arm; mobile app paired via BLE 5.0; network monitor open; simulator descending at -1 mg/dL/min
test_cases.steps:
Step: 1. Run trace for 5 minutes.
Step: 2. Inspect arrow display.
test_cases.expectedResults:
ExpectedResult: 1. Computed rate = -1.
ExpectedResult: 2. No arrow displayed.

test_cases.description: Verify arrow clears when rate flattens
test_cases.setup: downward arrow active from prior step
test_cases.steps:
Step: 1. Drive simulator to flat 100 mg/dL trace.
Step: 2. Wait 5 minutes.
Step: 3. Inspect arrow display.
test_cases.expectedResults:
ExpectedResult: 1. Computed rate near 0.
ExpectedResult: 2. Arrow cleared.

test_cases.expectedResults:
